skill.name: ui-ux-pro-max
skill.description: Searchable UI/UX design knowledge for styles, palettes, typography, charts, product patterns, accessibility, and framework-specific interface guidance.
skill.summary: Search UI/UX design references and stack-specific guidance before planning or building interfaces.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical matches, but review shows most are CSV examples, documentation URLs, and UI terminology. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found. The remaining concern is that this community skill asks agents to execute bundled Python scripts and includes package installation commands.

medium_findings.title: Community Skill Executes Bundled Python Search Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run a bundled Python search script. The reviewed code loads local CSV files and prints matching design guidance, but executing community code still requires user trust.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The execution path is explicit and the script behavior appears limited to local CSV reads and formatted output. Risk remains because it is executable community code.

medium_findings.title: Package Installation Commands Require User Approval
medium_findings.description: The prerequisite section includes brew, sudo apt, and winget commands for installing Python. These are visible setup commands, not stealth behavior, but package installation changes the host system.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly documented and are limited to Python installation. The sudo command creates elevated installation risk if an agent runs it without user confirmation.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Documentation and Framework Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs and fetch references are documentation links, Google Fonts links, and framework example snippets in CSV reference data. The Python scripts do not execute these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are reference rows used by the search database. No reviewed script performs outbound network requests from these values.

low_findings.title: Environment Secret Findings Are Educational Guidance
low_findings.description: The environment variable hits describe safe framework practices, such as not exposing API_SECRET or DATABASE_URL to client code. They are not real credential reads by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The variables appear inside CSV guidance columns and examples. The executable scripts only read CSV rows as text.

low_findings.title: Keylogger Keyword Findings Are Benign UI Wording
low_findings.description: The keylogger keyword matches come from form validation, input debounce, and native bridge guidance. They do not describe capturing or transmitting user keystrokes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matches are ordinary UI and performance terminology in educational CSV rows. No code path hooks keyboard events for collection or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: A targeted search for override, pre-approval, skip-review, and system-instruction phrases found no matching prompt injection text in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The visible skill introduction is normal metadata and usage description. No evidence found for instructions that try to override the audit process.

dangerous_patterns.title: Visible External Command Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell and PowerShell command examples for checking or installing Python and running search.py. These commands are not obfuscated, but they should require explicit user approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Static command findings are real, but the context is setup and local search usage. There is no hidden payload or suspicious command chaining beyond package installation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Heuristic Combination Dismissed After Context Review
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner combined network, filesystem, and credential keywords across separate documentation rows. Review found CSV examples and local file reads, not a single exfiltration flow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence spans unrelated local CSV loading and educational code examples. No reviewed code sends secrets or local file contents to a network endpoint.

content.user_title: Design Better UI With Stack Guidance
content.value_statement: Interface work often lacks a clear design system, accessible patterns, and stack-specific implementation guidance. This skill searches a curated UI/UX reference base and returns practical direction before planning, building, or reviewing UI.
actual_capabilities: Searches local CSV references for UI styles, product patterns, colors, typography, charts, and UX guidelines.
Returns stack-specific guidance for React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, and Tailwind HTML.
Recommends color palettes, font pairings, chart types, landing structures, and interaction patterns.
Supports UI planning, implementation guidance, review checklists, and improvement suggestions.
Uses a local BM25 search script to rank relevant design guidance from bundled data files.
limitations: The design knowledge is static and may not reflect the newest framework releases or design trends.
The skill requires Python to run its local search script.
It provides guidance and examples, but it does not verify rendered UI quality by itself.
External documentation URLs in the data are references only and may change over time.

use_cases.title: Plan a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Choose a style, palette, typography direction, and page structure before starting a new product screen.

use_cases.title: Build Frontend UI Faster
use_cases.description: Get stack-specific guidance for layout, state, accessibility, performance, and framework conventions.

use_cases.title: Review Existing Screens
use_cases.description: Check UI work against practical rules for contrast, spacing, responsiveness, interaction, and accessibility.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Visual Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max to recommend a style, color palette, typography pair, and layout direction for a healthcare landing page.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Screen for a Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max for a Next.js SaaS dashboard. Find relevant product, chart, UX, typography, and stack guidance before proposing the screen design.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max to review this React pricing page for spacing, contrast, hierarchy, accessibility, and conversion quality.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Complete Design System Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max to build a design system brief for a fintech analytics product, including style rationale, palette, typography, charts, UX risks, and React guidance.

output_examples.input: Plan a landing page for a premium spa service.
output_examples.output: A concise direction with a luxury visual style, warm neutral palette, elegant serif heading font, testimonial-led layout, and accessibility notes.
A prioritized review covering chart choice, loading states, responsive layout, contrast, environment handling guidance, and Next.js rendering patterns.
A focused checklist for touch targets, form labels, error states, keyboard behavior, trust cues, and React Native implementation concerns.

best_practices: Search several domains before making a design decision, such as product, style, color, typography, UX, and stack.
Treat generated guidance as a starting point and validate the final UI with real viewport, contrast, and keyboard checks.
Ask for stack-specific guidance when implementation details matter, especially for routing, forms, data fetching, and accessibility.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on one style result without checking accessibility, product fit, and implementation limits.
Do not let the agent run package installation commands without explicit user approval.
Do not copy framework examples blindly into production without validating security, state, and data boundaries.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan, build, and review UI using searchable design references and stack-specific frontend guidance.

faq.question: Does it generate finished designs automatically?
faq.answer: It provides structured guidance and examples. The agent still needs to apply the guidance to the actual project files.

faq.question: Which frontend stacks are covered?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Nuxt, Nuxt UI, and Tailwind HTML.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: The local search works from bundled CSV files. Documentation URLs in the data are references and are not required for search.

faq.question: Why is the security risk marked medium?
faq.answer: The skill asks agents to execute local Python scripts and includes package installation commands. The reviewed code did not show malicious behavior.

faq.question: Can it help with accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for contrast, forms, labels, focus behavior, responsive layouts, and platform-specific accessibility patterns.
